title: Protecting Electronics: How to Pack Smart TVs and Home Theater Systems for Bumpy Cobblestone Roads
excerpt: Pack smart TVs and home theater: cobblestone roads Addis, shock protection, original boxes, bubble wrap, electronics moving, fragile AV equipment.
content:
Protecting electronics: how to pack smart TVs and home theater systems for bumpy cobblestone roads

Piassa cobblestone and utility trench humps turn truck ride into low-frequency jackhammer—smart TV panels flex, speaker cones tear, HDMI ports shear if packed like books. Electronics demand original geometry, vertical travel, and shock isolation—not hope and blanket.

This guide packs smart TVs and home theater for Addis cobblestone routes.

Smart TV packing

  • Original box with foam inserts—best; if lost, TV carton with corner protectors.
  • Vertical orientation; screen face flat against rigid panel—never pressure on face.
  • Tape remote and stand hardware to box side in bag.
  • Photo cable routing before disconnect—setup speed at destination.

Home theater components

Receiver and amplifier in double-box with two-inch gap; disc drive empty. Speakers: woofer cones padded; tweeters especially fragile—mark NO STACK.

Cables and accessories

Label each HDMI by source; coil without sharp bend; bag together per system—not one spaghetti carton.

Truck placement

Electronics between mattresses or foam wall—mid-truck not tailgate slam zone. Strap so no slide into metal track.

Climate and dust

Bag seal against dust; avoid trunk with fuel smell; AC off long haul—condensation rare but wipe before power on.

Insurance and value

Declare TV size and model; owner-packed may limit claim—vendor pack for high value.

Destination acclimation

Wait thirty minutes from cold truck to warm room before plug-in—reduces condensation risk on boards.

Active Movers AV protocol

Disclose screen sizes at survey—corner protectors stocked on request.

Conclusion

Pack electronics for cobblestone with original cartons, vertical TVs, double-box receivers, mid-truck placement—home theater survives Addis roads when physics respected, not blankets alone.
author: Active Movers Team
